AggregatedObservation.description: Multiple user observations clustered into a single consensus point.
AggregatedObservation.title: AggregatedObservation

AvoidZoneRequest.description: A circular zone the router should try to avoid.
AvoidZoneRequest.title: AvoidZoneRequest

CheckoutV2Request.description: v2 tier-picker checkout. Client names the tier; server picks the price.

tier.description: Tier to purchase. `free` is rejected with 400.

CheckoutV2Request.title: CheckoutV2Request

ElevationResponse.description: Elevation profile with optional pre-computed grade segments.
ElevationResponse.title: ElevationResponse

EntitlementResponse.description:
Current effective entitlement for the authenticated user.

`tier=free` with `expires_at=null` and `source_platform=null` is the
no-purchase shape.

product_id.description: The platform product identifier that granted this tier. Null for `free` and for `legacy` grandfather rows.
product_id.title: Product Id

source_platform.description: Where the entitlement was purchased. `legacy` means it came from the v1 `user_entitlements` grandfather path.

tier.description: The user's current effective tier. `free` means no active pass and no grandfathered lifetime.

EntitlementResponse.title: EntitlementResponse

GradeSegment.description: Derived segment for elevation-aware fuel analysis.
GradeSegment.title: GradeSegment

GuideAction.description: Structured UI action rendered as a button/pill in the chat.
GuideAction.title: GuideAction

GuideSource.description: A web source the guide consulted this turn, surfaced for trust.
GuideSource.title: GuideSource

NearbyRoamer.description: A projected roamer position returned to the querying user.
NearbyRoamer.title: NearbyRoamer

PeerSyncDelta.description: Delta payload: only overlay items newer than the caller's timestamps.
PeerSyncDelta.title: PeerSyncDelta

PeerSyncRequest.description: Request a delta of overlay data newer than the caller's timestamps.
PeerSyncRequest.title: PeerSyncRequest

RedeemRequest.description:
Client-supplied receipt for server verification.

The `receipt` shape is platform-specific (Apple `signedTransactionInfo`
JWS, Google purchase token + product id, or Stripe Checkout Session id).
The receipt is opaque to the request schema; verification happens in the
platform-specific service layer.

platform.description: Where the purchase was made. `legacy` is not accepted by the redeem endpoint - it is a server-side synthesis only.

receipt.description: Platform-specific verification payload. iOS: `{signed_transaction_info: <JWS>}`. Android: `{purchase_token: str, product_id: str}`. Web: `{stripe_session_id: str}`.
receipt.title: Receipt

RedeemRequest.title: RedeemRequest

RedeemResponse.description: Result of a redeem call - same shape as GET /entitlement after success.

entitlement.description: The user's entitlement after the redeem attempt. On `granted=false` this is the prior tier unchanged.

grandfathered.description: True when the receipt named the legacy `roam_unlimited` SKU and the server granted Lifetime as a result.
grandfathered.title: Grandfathered

granted.description: True when the receipt verified and an entitlement row was written or matched an existing row by (platform, transaction_id).
granted.title: Granted

RedeemResponse.title: RedeemResponse

coverage_note.default: NSW only - data from NSW BioNet Animal Vehicle Strike dataset
coverage_note.title: Coverage Note

SourcePlatform.description:
Where the purchase was made (and which receipt verifier to trust).

`legacy` is synthesised by the read path when the user has only a v1
`user_entitlements` row (binary unlock, pre-v2). It never appears in the
`entitlements` table itself.
SourcePlatform.title: SourcePlatform

StopSuggestionItem.description: A single suggested place to add as a trip stop.
StopSuggestionItem.title: StopSuggestionItem

StopSuggestionsRequest.description:
Request nearby POI suggestions for the trip stop list.

Uses the trip bounding box for Overpass queries, then scores candidates
by proximity to the route midpoint and category diversity vs existing stops.
StopSuggestionsRequest.title: StopSuggestionsRequest

Tier.description:
User entitlement tier.

`free` is the absence-of-row default; no row in the `entitlements` table
ever stores `'free'`. The enum carries it so the GET endpoint can return a
typed value rather than null.
Tier.title: Tier

TripProgress.description: Live position + progress telemetry sent from frontend.
TripProgress.title: TripProgress

WirePlace.description:
Pre-filtered "relevant places" the server hands to the LLM so it
can recommend without a tool call.  Includes contact info for
action buttons.
WirePlace.title: WirePlace

delete.description:
Permanently delete the authenticated user's account and all associated data.

Supabase cascade rules on auth.users ensure all rows in user_entitlements,
user_trip_counts, saved_places, roam_plans, roam_plan_members,
emergency_contacts, and stop_memories are removed automatically.

post.description: Re-fetch traffic & hazards, recompute and cache the route intelligence score.

200.description: Offline trip bundle zip (manifest + tiles + corridor + places + fuel)

get.description:
Resolve and return the authenticated user's current effective tier.

Source of truth for all three native clients (`glovebox-ios`,
`glovebox-android`, `glovebox-web`). Resolution order is in
`app/services/entitlements.py`. The endpoint is cheap (one indexed read,
optional second read for the legacy grandfather check) and called on
every cold start of the v2 clients plus before any paywalled action.

post.description:
Verify a platform-specific receipt and grant the corresponding tier.

iOS and Android receipts are verified server-to-server against Apple's
JWS public keys / Google's Play Developer API. The web path is
deliberately rejected: web purchases flow through the Stripe Checkout +
webhook path, never through this endpoint.

Grandfather behaviour: when the verified receipt names the legacy
`roam_unlimited` SKU, the server grants `Tier.LIFETIME` regardless of
the tier the client suggested. The response's `grandfathered=True`
flag tells the client to update its UI without surprise.

Idempotency: the `(source_platform, transaction_id)` unique index on
`public.entitlements` absorbs duplicate redemptions from flaky clients
or auto-retries. The endpoint always returns the user's current
effective entitlement after the call, so a duplicate redeem returns
the same `granted=True` shape as the first one.

get.description:
Liveness probe - always 200 while the process is up.
Fly.io and load balancers use this to decide whether to route traffic.

post.description: Query aggregated observations near a position.

Build a delta of overlay data newer than the caller's timestamps.
Used when roamers exchange data via BLE or when the app regains signal.

Return up to req.limit nearby POI suggestions for the trip stop list.

Queries Overpass within the trip bounding box, scores candidates by
proximity to the route midpoint and category diversity, returns top N.
Find other roamers predicted to be nearby (dead-reckoning).
Upsert the user's latest known position.

get.description:
Readiness probe - checks that the cache DB is queryable.
Returns 503 if the DB is not ready so Fly.io stops routing traffic.

post.description:
Handles both Stripe and RevenueCat webhooks.

Stripe sends a stripe-signature header; RevenueCat does not.

Webhook bodies are vendor-defined and not modelled at the route signature
layer; signature verification needs the raw request body, so Request stays.

404.description: Tile not found or missing on disk
